mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals a sharp labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike exceeding the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and housing market strength, which jointly drive corporate revenue growth and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an increase in the industrial production index reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO signals during tightening cycles produce negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances across the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.5% year-over-year threshold, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold, invalidating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows, indicating no additive labor effect.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20d forward returns (p-value > 0.10) across the full historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals a sudden supply-side contraction that raises recession probabilities, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances following a >1.0% monthly drop in industrial production, refuting the contraction signal.
